documentation:
# PyAnten

Antenna Toolkit

## Installation

```shell
pip install pyanten
```

## Table of Modules and Functions

| Module      | Sub Module (Level1) | Sub Module (Level2) | Functinos                                                                                                                           |
| ----------- | ------------------- | ------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Utility     |                     |                     | frequencyToWavelength, gammaDbToSwr, gammaToSwr, swrToGamma, swrToGammaDb, wavelengthToFrequency, gammaDbToReflectedPowerPercentage |
| Propagation |                     |                     | farfFieldDistance, lineOfSight, pathLoss, radioHorizon,                                                                             |
| Measurement | NearField           |                     | minimumSeperation,                                                                                                                       |
| Measurement | NearField           | Planar              | angleOfView, samplingParameters, samplingLength, scanLength,                                                                             |
| Measurement | NearField           | Spherical           | maximumRadiusExtent,                                                                                                                |
| Array       |                     |                     | amplitudeTaper,                                                                                                                     |

## References

[1] [A Python MoM antenna simulator](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/python-mom-antenna-simulator-t-q-khai-nguyen/)  
[2] [How to document kwargs according to Numpy style docstring?](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/62511086/how-to-document-kwargs-according-to-numpy-style-docstring)

functions.name: pyanten.Propagation.lineOfSight.lineOfSight
documentation:
Line-of-sight range in kilometers [km] for a given height

# Parameters

height

: height of transmitter/recevier above ground in meters [m]

# Returns

LOS

: line-of-sight distance in kilometers [km]

# References

[^footnote-1]: \[Line-of-sight propagation - Wikipedia\](<https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Line-of-sight_propagation>)

# Formula

$$
R_{los} = \sqrt{2\cdotR\cdoth} \approx 3.57\times\sqrt{h} [km]
$$

functions.name: pyanten.Propagation.farFieldDistance.farFieldDistance
documentation:
Far-field (Fraunhofer) distance of an antenna or aperture

# Parameters

frequency

: frequency of interest in Hertz [Hz]

D

: maximum cross-sectional size [m]

# Returns

Rff

: minimum far-field range in meters [m]

# References

[^footnote-1]: IEEE 1720-2012 Recommended Practice for Near-Field Antenna Measurements, Section B.2.2, Page 175, Equation B.6.

[^footnote-2]: Warren L. Stutzman, Antenna Theory and Design, 3rd Ed., Page 43.

# Formula

$$
R_{ff} = Max\left(\dfrac{2D^2}{\lambda},5D,1.6\lambda\right)
$$

functions.name: pyanten.Utility.swrToGammaDb.swrToGammaDb
documentation:
Voltage standing wave ratio (vswr) to reflection coefficient (gamma) in decibel conversion

Parameters
----------
swr : float
    standing wave ratio (swr)

Returns
-------
gamma : float
    reflection coefficient (s11) [dB]

functions.name: pyanten.Propagation.radioHorizon.radioHorizon
documentation:
Radio horizon in kilometers [km] for a given height

# Parameters

height

: height of transmitter/recevier above ground in meters [m]

# Returns

Rh

: radio horizon in kilometers [km]

# References

[^footnote-1]: \[Line-of-sight propagation - Wikipedia\](<https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Line-of-sight_propagation>)

# Formula

$$
R_{rh} \approx 4.12\times\sqrt{h} [km]
$$

Converts frequency to wavelength

functions.name: pyanten.Propagation.pathLoss.pathLoss
documentation:
Free space path loss under far-field condition

# Parameters

frequency

: frequency of interest in MegaHertz [MHz]

R

: range in kilometers [km]

G1

: gain of transmitter antenna in decibels [dB]

G2

: gain of receiver antenna in decibels [dB]

# Returns

Lp

: path loss in decibels [dB]

# Notes

This calculation valid if two antennas are seperated enough to be inside far-field (Fraunhofer) region.

# References

[^footnote-1]: IEEE 1720-2012 Recommended Practice for Near-Field Antenna Measurements, Section B.2.2, Page 175, Equation B.6.

[^footnote-2]: Warren L. Stutzman, Antenna Theory and Design, 3rd Ed., Page 43.

# Formula

$$
L_P = 32.45 + 20\log10(fR) - G_1 - G_2 \quad [dB]
$$

functions.name: pyanten.Measurement.NearField.Planar.samplingLength.samplingLength
documentation:
Maximum sampling length for near-field antenna measurements

# Parameters

frequency

: frequency of interest in Hertz [Hz]

# References

[^footnote-1]: IEEE 149-2021 Recommended Practice for Antenna Measurements, Section 12.5, Page 134.

[^footnote-2]: IEEE 1720-2012 Recommended Practice for Near-Field Antenna Measurements, Section 5.2.5, Page 23, Equation 25.

# Formula

$$
\Delta = \lambda / 2 = 0.5 \times (c_0 / f)
$$

functions.name: pyanten.Measurement.NearField.Planar.samplingParameters.samplingParameters
documentation:
Planar near-field antenna measurement sampling count according to frequency and desired minimum length

# Parameters

frequency

: frequency of interest in Hertz [Hz]

L

: desired minimum scan length [m]

# Returns

Lm

: sampling start position [m]

Lp

: sampling stop position [m] (equals to negative of Lp)

N

: samplng count

Delta

: spatial samplng length [mm]

# Notes

Calculation assumes a scanning region centered on the AUT.

# Formula

$$
theta = \tan^{-1} \left \dfrac{L-a}{2d} \right
$$

functions.name: pyanten.Measurement.NearField.Planar.angleOfView.angleOfView
documentation:
Reliable far-field angle-of-view in planar near-field antenna measurements

# Parameters

a

: antenna cross-section length

d

: seperation distance between antenna and probe

L

: scan length of region

# Notes

Calculation assumes a scanning region centered on the AUT.
Input distances are unitless but should in same quantity

# References

[^footnote-1]: IEEE 149-2021 Recommended Practice for Antenna Measurements, Section 12.5, Page 135, Equation 99.

[^footnote-2]: IEEE 1720-2012 Recommended Practice for Near-Field Antenna Measurements, Section 5.3.1.6, Page 28, Equation 27.

# Formula

$$
theta = \tan^{-1} \left \dfrac{L-a}{2d} \right
$$

functions.name: pyanten.Measurement.NearField.minimumSeperation.minimumSeperation
documentation:
Minimum recommended distance for near-field antenna measurements in meters

# Parameters

frequency

: frequency of interest in Hertz [Hz]

closer

: closer approximation using 3 wavelength calculation

# Returns

float

: minimum distance

# Notes

Standard suggests to choose between 3 or 5 wavelength.
In order to ensure copuling effect, 5 wavelength distance is choosen and implemented. 3 wavelength is optional.

[^footnote-1]: IEEE 1720-2012 Recommended Practice for Near-Field Antenna Measurements, Section 5.3.1.4, Page 27.

# Formula

$$
R_{nf} = 5\times\lambda = 5\times c_0/f
$$

functions.name: pyanten.Measurement.NearField.Planar.scanLength.scanLength
documentation:
Required length of the scan for desired angle-of-view in planar near-field antenna measurements

# Parameters

a

: antenna cross-section length

d

: seperation distance between antenna and probe

theta

: desired pattern view angle along one side [deg]

# Notes

Calculation assumes a scanning region centered on the AUT.
Input distances are unitless so output is the same quantity of inputs.

# References

[^footnote-1]: IEEE 149-2021 Recommended Practice for Antenna Measurements, Section 12.5, Page 135, Equation 99.

[^footnote-2]: IEEE 1720-2012 Recommended Practice for Near-Field Antenna Measurements, Section 5.3.1.6, Page 28, Equation 27.

# Formula

$$
L = 2d \cdot \tan\theta + a
$$

functions.name: pyanten.Measurement.NearField.Spherical.maximumRadiusExtent.maximumRadiusExtent
documentation:
Calculates maximum radial extend (MRE) of the antenna for spherical near-field (SNF) measurements

# Parameters

dx

: max displacement from rotation center along x-axis

dy

: max displacement from rotation center along y-axis

dz

: max displacement from rotation center along z-axis

# Returns

mre

: maximum radius extent

# Notes

Calculation is unitless so output is the same quantity of inputs.

# Formula

$$
mre = \sqrt{dx^2+dy^2+dz^2}
$$

functions.name: pyanten.Array.amplitudeTaper.amplitudeTaper
documentation:
Generate amplitude taper coefficients for antenna elements in sidelobe controlle array 

  Parameters
  ----------
  N      : int 
           number of array elements
  sll    : float
           desired sidelobe level [dB]
  taper  : str 
           window function associated to desired distribution that can be:
           taylor,
           dolhp-chebyshev / chebyshev / tchebyshev 
**kwargs : dict
           window specific properties. Valid keyword arguments are: 

           taylor_nbar: int: Number of nearly constant-level sidelobes adjacent to the mainlobe in taylor window (default=4)
         
  Returns
  -------
  amplitudes : float[]
               amplitude array corresponds to each element

  References
  ----------
  .. [1] [taylor — SciPy v1.14.1 Manual](https://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy/reference/generated/scipy.signal.windows.taylor.html)
  .. [1] [chebwin — SciPy v1.14.1 Manual](https://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy/reference/generated/scipy.signal.windows.chebwin.html)
  .. [2] [Armin Doerry, “Catalog of Window Taper Functions for Sidelobe Control”, 2017](https://www.osti.gov/biblio/1365510)
  .. [3] [Sayedu Khasim, Noorbasha & Krishna Y, Murali & Thati, Jagadeesh & Subbarao, M.. (2013). ANALYSIS OF DIFFERENT TAPERING TECHNIQUES FOR EFFICIENT RADIATION PATTERN. e-Journal of Science & Technology (e-JST). 8. 47-55. ](https://www.dl.begellhouse.com/journals/0632a9d54950b268,4b96ebea37254651,10b28d794a40eb40.html)
